sections.paragraph:
ἐξιλάσκομαι (s. ἱλάσκομαι) fut. ἐξιλάσομαι; 1 aor. ἐξιλασάμην; fut. pass. 3 sg. ἐξιλασθήσεται LXX. When one endeavors to attain the goodwill of another, the word can be rendered appease (a pers. PTebt 750, 16 [II b.c.]); in this sense ἐ. is akin to θεραπεύειν as in Thu 1, 137, 3 τινὰ χρημάτων δόσει. Throughout Greek lit., deity is the most freq. obj. (as early as the oracle in Hdt. 7, 141 Δία; X., Cyr. 7, 2, 19 Ἀπόλλωνα; Menand., Fgm.754, 6 Kö. [=544, 6 p. 164 Kock] τὸν θεόν; Polyb. 3, 112, 19; Diod S 1, 59, 2 θυσίαις τὸ θεῖον; 14, 77, 4 τ. θεούς; 20, 14, 3; Strabo 4, 1, 13; Dio Chrys. 15 [32], 77; Ael. Aristid. 46, 3 K.=3 p. 30 D.; Zech 7:2; 8:22; Ezk 16:63; PsSol 3:8; TestLevi 3:5 πρὸς κύριον; JosAs 28:5; EpArist 316; Philo, Poster. Cai. 72 [after Lev 16:10]; Jos., Bell. 5, 19; SibOr 7, 30.—S. ἱλάσκομαι and ἵλεως). In 1 Cl 7:7; Hv 1, 2, 1 sin necessitates appeasement of God (cp. IG II2, 1366, 15f=SIG 1042 [II/III a.d.] ἁμαρτίαν ὀφειλέτω Μηνὶ Τυράννῳ, ἣν οὐ μὴ δύνηται ἐξειλάσασθαι [= let such a one be liable to Sovereign Men for an offense for which there is no expiation]; s. also Just., A I, 50, 2 τοῖς ἀνόμοις ἐξιλάσεται [for Is 53:12]).—TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. sg. = singular

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

pers. pers. = person(s)

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

obj. obj. = object, objective

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Kock Kock , s. Com.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Dio Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 350
